page: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. This configuration does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark evaluations place the D96as_v7 in the top tier (top 10%) for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance under stress-ng testing. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with small-block cached reads and large-block uncached writes ranking in the top 10%, while large-block uncached reads perform in the top 25%. The system demonstrates exceptional processing capabilities in data manipulation tasks, achieving top-tier results in gzip compression, bzip3 decompression, and Passmark database operations. Additionally, it handles Redis SET operations at 138.89 million operations per second and static web serving at 92.8 GB/sec.

For machine learning workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference speeds for text generation remain in the top tier across small, medium, and large models, though prompt processing for a large 70B model drops to average performance. Given the high compute density and balanced memory-to-core ratio, this server is highly cost-efficient for resource-intensive general-purpose workloads. It is well-suited for enterprise database hosting, high-throughput web serving, data compression pipelines, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture at 4.5 GHz, it features 96 vCPUs (48 physical cores) and 384.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB per core. The instance includes no local storage or GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks place this server in the top 10% for single-core and multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth. It delivers high throughput for data compression, Redis operations, and static web serving. CPU-based LLM inference also ranks in the top tier for text generation. The D96as_v7 is optimized for enterprise databases, high-throughput web servers, and multi-threaded computational workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and top-tier CPU and memory performance for enterprise workloads.

tagline: A high-core AMD EPYC virtual machine delivering balanced memory and top-tier compute performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
